Off Calendar Apr-26-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Matthew P. James, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Rubecon General Contracting,Inc.
David F. Mangini, Atty. for Defendant and opposing party: Apartment Investment

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF RUBECON GENERAL CONTRACTING INC A CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, Against Apartment Investment And Management Company a/k/a Aimco is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as the court did not make a ruling that all privileges had been waived. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity within 10 days of notice of this order. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 4 as the response is inadequate. The qualifying language about what Plaintiff would or would not have done is improper. If the response is that Plaintiff did not request the drawing from the responding parties, that response must be given after reasonable and good faith effort has been made to obtain the information by inquiry to other persons or organizations. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
